Buy aromatic plants online for your home garden from The Affordable Organic Store. These plants have numerous benefits and can be given as gifts to your loved ones. Plant saplings are easy to care for and require low maintenance as they grow steadily. Free shipping above Rs.150. With the convenience of online seeds, enthusiasts and green thumbs alike have easy access to a wide variety of plant options, including herb seeds. These aromatic wonders are now at your fingertips, allowing you to embark on a fragrant journey of growth, wellness, and culinary delights right from your own home. we will delve into the world of online seeds unlocking the potential to cultivate and enjoy nature’s fragrant treasures in a new, convenient way.

What is an aromatic plant?

Aromatic plants are those that contain aromatic compounds – basically, essential oils that are volatile at room temperature. These essential oils are odorous, volatile, hydrophobic, and highly concentrated compounds. These types of plants are often cultivated for their essential oils.t

Read More

Why is it called aromatic?

Aromatics, so called because of their distinctive perfumed smell, are substances derived from crude oil and, in small quantities, from coal. Aromatics are hydrocarbons, organic compounds that consist exclusively of the elements carbon and hydrogen – without which life would not be possible on Earth.

Importance of aromatic plants

Aromatic plants provide essential oil which is extracted for various applications in industries. For example, the extracts of these aromatic plants are used in cosmetics for flavoring and fragrance purposes.

Features of aromatic plants

Aromatic plants are those that contain aromatic compounds – basically, essential oils that are volatile at room temperature. These essential oils are odorous, volatile, hydrophobic, and highly concentrated compounds. They can be obtained from flowers, buds, seeds, leaves, twigs, bark, wood, fruits, and roots

Best time to grow aromatic plants

Although aromatic plants can thrive in poor soil, when growing in pots it is essential to fertilize and/or feed them properly. This is usually done on a regular basis in the warm season from March to October. Over-fertilization should be avoided, as this will cause them to lose flavor and aroma.

How do I harvest and dry aromatic fragrant plants?

Harvesting is usually done during the dry season (December to February) by manually digging out the bush along with its roots. The roots are then separated from the leaves, washed, and dried under the shade for 1-2 days before distillation.

Extracting fragrance from plants

Distillation is a common technique for obtaining aromatic compounds from plants, such as orange blossoms and roses. The raw material is heated and the fragrant compounds are re-collected through condensation of the distilled vapor.

Aromatic fragrant plants that can be used as natural insect repellents

Aromatic fragrant plants can act as natural insect repellents due to their strong scents, which deter bugs. This makes them an effective and chemical-free way to keep insects away from your surroundings.

Methods of aroma extraction

Essential oils are the liquids that are isolated from plants when introduced to solvents – they are liquefied versions of the plants! Popular extraction methods include Steam Distillation, Solvent Extraction, CO2 Extraction, Maceration, Enfleurage, Cold Press Extraction, and Water Distillation.

Common problems that can affect aromatic fragrant plants

Aromatic fragrant plants may face common issues like overwatering, poor soil drainage, and pests. Overwatering can lead to root rot, while inadequate drainage can cause root issues. Pests, like aphids or spider mites, can damage the plants and their scents. Proper care helps prevent these problems.

How to fertilize aromatic plants?

You can use organic fertilizer, such as manure, guano, or similar. If it is mineral fertilizer, dissolve it in the irrigation once a month. Another good technique is to change the first 4 centimeters of the substrate to a peat-enriched substrate once a year.

How do I ensure my aromatic fragrant plants receive enough sunlight?

To provide adequate sunlight for your aromatic fragrant plants, choose a sunny spot with at least 6 hours of direct sunlight daily. South-facing windows or well-lit outdoor locations work well. Regularly check and adjust their position to ensure they receive the required sunlight for their health and fragrance.

Medicinally important aromatic plants

Aromatic plants have been used as natural medicines since prehistoric times. Plants with medicinal or aromatic properties that are used in pharmacy and/or perfumery are usually defined as medicinal and aromatic plants. Antioxidant and antimicrobial activities of aromatic plants have been widely explored and found to have health applications in prevention and reducing the risk of diseases such as inflammation, atherosclerosis, cardiovascular and cancer.

Examples of aromatic plants

Aromatic plants, like perennial culinary herbs (rosemary and lavender) and many other plants, produce essential oils that are released when touching the foliage and flowers. Aromatic plants are rabbit and deer-resistant because the aromatic oils are very bitter.

Seven aromatic vegetables

Popular aromatic vegetables include onions, shallots, scallions, celery, garlic, carrots, leeks, and bell peppers. For fast-cooking recipes, dice the veggies, and for slow-cooking recipes, like stock, you can use large cuts.

Aromatics in cooking

Aromatics are vegetables, herbs, and spices that add aroma and flavor to dishes. Use various combinations of aromatics for cooking food from different cuisines.  Indian cuisine features many spices for curries, meat, and vegetable dishes. Indian recipes generally begin by heating spices such as cumin, cardamom, turmeric, and cloves in oil or ghee. Onions, tomatoes, ginger, and yogurt are common flavorings in addition to aromatics.

Flowers that are most fragrant

  • Hyacinth
  • Freesia
  • Rose
  • Peony
  • Sweet Pea
  • Lily

In conclusion, aromatic plants offer a world of possibilities for gardeners. With the convenience of online seeds, nurturing these fragrant wonders has become more accessible than ever. These plants not only enhance the aesthetics of your garden but also provide natural pest control and aromatic pleasures. Embrace the scents of nature and let aromatic plants elevate your gardening experience.

settings.faq_title: What are aromatic fragrant plants?
settings.faq_content: Plants with a nice scent or aroma are known as aromatic plants. They are frequently employed in herbal medicine, cooking, and aromatherapy.

settings.faq_title: What are some common types of aromatic fragrant plants?
settings.faq_content: Lavender, rosemary, thyme, mint, basil, and jasmine are a few common examples of aromatic fragrant plants.

settings.faq_title: How do I care for my aromatic fragrant plants?
settings.faq_content: Aromatic plants need regular watering, soil that drains well, and lots of sunlight. Additionally, regular fertilisation and trimming can support their health.

settings.faq_title: How do I propagate my aromatic fragrant plants?
settings.faq_content: Cuttings, division, or seed can all be used to multiply aromatic fragrant plants.

settings.faq_title: What is the best time to plant aromatic fragrant plants?
settings.faq_content: Depending on the species and your region, spring or autumn are the best times to plant fragrant aromatic plants.

settings.faq_title: How do I choose the right aromatic fragrant plants for my garden?
settings.faq_content: Consider your particular preferences for scent and aesthetics, the size of your garden, how much sunlight it receives, and other factors when selecting aromatic fragrant plants.

settings.faq_title: What is the difference between annual and perennial aromatic fragrant plants?
settings.faq_content: While perennial aromatic fragrant plants can thrive for many years, annual aromatic fragrant plants only have one growing season.

settings.faq_title: How do I protect my aromatic fragrant plants from pests and diseases?
settings.faq_content: Keep your fragrant aromatic plants clean and well-maintained, and get rid of any sick or infected plants right away to protect them from pests and illnesses.

settings.faq_title: Can aromatic fragrant plants be used as natural remedies for common ailments?
settings.faq_content: Yes, many fragrant plants can be used as natural cures for common maladies including headaches, colds, and digestive problems due to their medicinal characteristics.

settings.faq_title: How do I harvest and dry aromatic fragrant plants?
settings.faq_content: Cut the stems of aromatic fragrant plants, then hang them up to dry completely in a cool, dry spot. Then keep them free from moisture and light in sealed containers.

settings.faq_title: How do I make essential oils from aromatic fragrant plants?
settings.faq_content: Steam distillation or cold pressing are two methods that can be used to extract essential oils from aromatic, fragrant plants. Utilise top-notch tools and supplies, and adhere to proper safety practises.

settings.faq_title: How do I use aromatic fragrant plants in beauty and skincare products?
settings.faq_content: Soaps, lotions, and bath salts are just a few of the cosmetics and skincare items that can use aromatic fragrance herbs.

settings.faq_title: Can aromatic fragrant plants be used as natural insect repellents?
settings.faq_content: Yes, many fragrant aromatic plants have the ability to repel insects, making them useful for preventing pests like flies and mosquitoes from congregating in outdoor areas.

settings.faq_title: How do I design a garden with aromatic fragrant plants?
settings.faq_content: Consider the colours, textures, and aromas of the plants as well as how they will compliment one another when planning a garden with aromatic fragrant plants. Consider the garden’s location and intended use, such as a meditation retreat or an area for outdoor entertaining.

settings.faq_title: Can aromatic fragrant plants be used in potpourri and sachets?
settings.faq_content: Indeed, aromatic plants are frequently used to make potpourri and sachets, which may be stuffed into drawers or closets to provide a scented touch.

settings.faq_title: How do I make herbal teas from aromatic fragrant plants?
settings.faq_content: The leaves or blossoms of aromatic fragrant plants can be steeped in hot water for several minutes to create herbal teas. To add more flavour, you can add honey or lemon.

settings.faq_title: Can aromatic fragrant plants be used in cooking for pets?
settings.faq_content: It is crucial to conduct research on which plants are safe and at what dosages before using any aromatic plants in pet food.

settings.faq_title: How do I create a natural air freshener using aromatic fragrant plants?
settings.faq_content: By simmering the leaves or petals of aromatic fragrant plants in water on the stove or using an essential oil diffuser, you can make a natural air freshener.

settings.faq_title: Can aromatic fragrant plants be grown hydroponically?
settings.faq_content: Yes, a lot of aromatic plants can be cultivated hydroponically, a technique that involves growing plants in nutrient-rich water instead of soil.

settings.faq_title: How do I choose the right soil for aromatic fragrant plants?
settings.faq_content: When selecting soil for aromatic plants, search for a potting mix that is well-draining and nutrient-rich and is especially made for the kind of plant you are cultivating.
